text: As artificial intelligence becomes more common, ethical questions are increasingly important. One key issue is bias, where AI systems can reflect and amplify existing prejudices in their training data. This can lead to unfair outcomes, for example in job applications or loan approvals. To ensure fairness, developers must carefully check their data and algorithms. Another concern is privacy. AI often collects vast amounts of personal information, so users need clear control over how their data is used. Transparency is also essential: people should understand why an AI makes certain decisions. Ultimately, responsible AI development means building systems that are fair, respect privacy, and are open about their workings.
